name: Stain Treatment Guide
description: A chemistry-based approach to stain removal: alkaline paste for grease, oxidizers for organic stains, chelating acids for rust/tannin, and UV for whites — matching the stain's chemistry to the correct treatment.
effectivenessNote: Targeted chemistry often outperforms generic commercial stain removers. Enzyme-based commercial products still have an edge on protein stains. UV/sunlight bleaching is genuinely one of the most effective whiteners.
warnings: Match chemistry to stain type — wrong treatment can set stains
Oxalic acid requires gloves and ventilation
Test on inconspicuous area first for colored fabrics
H₂O₂ can bleach colored fabrics — test first
scienceNote: Grease = alkaline saponification (washing soda). Blood/wine/coffee = oxidative bleaching (H₂O₂). Rust/tannin = chelation (oxalic acid). Yellowing = UV photolysis (sunlight). Different stains need different chemistry.
